metadata.description: Basic customization template for client governance

instructions: 2. Modify values as needed for your organization
3. Do NOT modify immutable core governance (marked with 🔒)
4. Ensure all OBRIGATÓRIO items are respected

GUARDRAIL.title: No bare exceptions in production code

original.title: No bare exceptions in production code
original.content:
# GUARDRAIL-001: No bare exceptions in production code

## Description

Bare `except:` or `except Exception:` clauses are forbidden in source code (except in tests).
Must catch specific exceptions.

## Implementation

```python
# ❌ BAD
try:
    result = dangerous_operation()
except:
    print("Error!")

# ✅ GOOD
try:
    result = dangerous_operation()
except ValueError as e:
    logger.error(f"Invalid value: {e}")
    raise CustomError(f"Could not process: {e}") from e
```

## Exceptions

- Tests may use bare `except` if needed
- Shell scripts may use catch-all

## Validation

```bash
grep -r "except:\|except Exception:" src/ && exit 1
pytest tests/quality/test_bare_exceptions.py -v
```

## Criticality

- OBRIGATÓRIO: In user-facing APIs
- ALERTA: In internal utilities
- OPCIONAL: In batch jobs

original.title: When to Customize
original.content: - ✅ Your domain has different scale needs (embedded vs cloud) - ✅ Your tech stack differs from Python/FastAPI - ✅ Your team has different constraints - ✅ You need fewer/more layers than the standard...

original.title: ✅ Immutable Core (DO NOT CHANGE)
original.content: These 5 principles must stay in every SDD Constitution: 1. **Clean separation of concerns** — Business logic isolated from infrastructure 2. **Explicit governance** — Rules documented, not implicit...

original.title: ✅ Flexible (You Choose)
original.content: These aspects can be customized: - **Number of layers** — 4-layer, 6-layer, 8-layer depending on your needs - **Specific ports** — What adapter contracts your domain needs - **Testing strategy** —...

original.title: Step 1: Choose Your Baseline
original.content: - Start with `/EXECUTION/spec/guides/adoption/templates/lite-constitution.yaml` - Modify the 5 principles for your domain - Takes: 15 minutes - Start with...

original.title: Step 2: Identify Your Constraints
original.content: Answer these questions: 1. **What's your scale?** - Solo dev → ULTRA-LITE - Small team (< 5) → LITE - Production system → FULL 2. **What's your primary I/O pattern?** - Async (web services) → Keep...

original.title: Step 3: Create Your Constitution
original.content: Create `.ai/constitution.md` in your project: ```markdown

original.title: Principle 1: Clean Separation
original.content: [Explain for your domain]

original.title: Principle 2: ...
original.content: [etc.]

original.title: Definition of Done (5 items)
original.content: - [ ] Feature implemented - [ ] Tests passing - [ ] [Your #3] - [ ] [Your #4] - [ ] [Your #5]

original.title: Customizations from SDD LITE
original.content: - [Async requirement waived because: embedded system] - [Simplified to 4-layer because: fixed infrastructure] - [Added: load testing requirement] - [Clean separation] - [Explicit rules] - [Test...

original.title: Step 4: Document Your Deviations
original.content: For each customization, explain: 1. **What** — What principle/rule did you change? 2. **Why** — Why was the standard version not suitable? 3. **How** — What's your alternative? 4. **Validation** —...

original.title: Customization: No Async-First
original.content: - Embedded system with single-threaded event loop - All I/O is sequential and blocking - Async would add unnecessary complexity - Single-threaded execution model - Clear sequential I/O operations -...

original.title: Step 5: Validate Your Custom Constitution
original.content: ```bash

original.title: Validate your constitution syntax
original.content: python -c \

GUIDELINE.title: Check your code matches the constitution

original.title: Check your code matches the constitution
original.content: pytest tests/constitution/

original.title: Example test:
original.content: def test_no_framework_in_domain(): validate customization no framework imports in domain

original.title: Step 6: Version Your Constitution
original.content: Track changes like you'd track code: ```markdown

original.title: v1.2 (2026-04-20)
original.content: - Added load testing requirement (Rule 3) - Increased concurrent entity requirement from 10 to 20

original.title: v1.1 (2026-04-15)
original.content: - Simplified to 4-layer architecture - Waived async-first for embedded system

original.title: v1.0 (2026-04-10)
original.content: - Initial custom constitution based on SDD LITE ``` ---

original.title: 1. Embedded System (No Async)
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Principle 1: Clean Separation
original.content: [Same as SDD]

original.title: Principle 2: Single-Threaded Execution
original.content: All code runs on a single thread with sequential I/O. Async/await is not used. Functions are synchronous.

original.title: Principle 3: Clear Ownership
original.content: Each module owns its resources. No shared mutable state.

original.title: Principle 4: ...
original.content: [Continue with SDD principles] ```

original.title: 3. Data Pipeline (Focus on Resilience)
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Rule 4: Checkpointing
original.content: Every pipeline stage has a checkpoint. If failure occurs, resume from last checkpoint (not restart).

original.title: Rule 5: Dead Letter Queue
original.content: Failed items go to DLQ for manual review, not retry loops. ```

original.title: 4. Microservice (Focus on Contract Testing)
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Rule X: API Contract Testing
original.content: Every endpoint has a contract test. Consumer contracts defined in OpenAPI/Pact. Producer validates against consumer contracts. ``` ---

original.title: Template: Custom Constitution
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Principle 1: [Name]
original.content: [Repeat for 5 principles]

original.title: Definition of Done
original.content: - [ ] Feature implemented - [ ] Tests passing - [ ] [Your #3] - [ ] [Your #4] - [ ] [Your #5]

original.title: Deviations from SDD
original.content: [List what you changed and why]

original.title: Validation
original.content: [How you verify this works]

original.title: Team Sign-Off
original.content: - [Name]: [Date] - [Name]: [Date]

original.title: References
original.content: - [SDD Baseline Document] - [Your project architecture] - [Additional standards] ``` ---

original.title: 🚨 Red Flags
original.content: If you find yourself doing these, **stop and reconsider:** - ❌ \

original.title: ✅ Signs of Good Customization
original.content: - ✅ Constitution is 2-4 pages - ✅ Every change is documented with rationale - ✅ Team agrees on it (sign-off document) - ✅ Validation is automated (tests or linting) - ✅ You can explain each...

original.title: Next Steps
original.content: 1. **Choose your baseline** — ULTRA-LITE, LITE, or FULL? 2. **Answer the 5 constraint questions** (Step 2) 3. **Create your constitution** (Step 3) 4. **Get team buy-in** (sign-off) 5. **Add...

original.title: Getting Help
original.content: | Component | Can Customize | Recommendation | |-----------|---------------|-----------------| | **Number of layers** | ✅ Yes | Document why (4, 6, or 8) | | **Specific rules** | ✅ Yes | Keep...

original.title: 📋 Overview
original.content: IA-FIRST (AI-First) is a markdown format designed for: - ✅ Machine parsing (AI agents, scripts) - ✅ Clarity (humans and machines) - ✅ Consistency (no format variations) - ✅ Efficiency (reduced...

GUIDELINE.title: Rule 1: Heading Hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3 → Lists)

original.title: Rule 1: Heading Hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3 → Lists)
original.content: ```markdown ✅ CORRECT:

original.title: Subsection (H3 — Sub-topics)
original.content: - Item 1 - Item 2 - Nested item ❌ WRONG:

original.title: Skipped levels (H4 — no H2/H3)
original.content: ❌ WRONG:

original.title: Prose text without heading
original.content: Some paragraph text here (shouldn't exist without H3) - Items follow prose ``` ---

GUIDELINE.title: Rule 2: Links Use Markdown Format ONLY

original.title: Rule 2: Links Use Markdown Format ONLY
Option A: Auto-Fix (Recommended)
original.content: This automatically: - ✅ Fixes heading hierarchy - ✅ Adds missing IA-FIRST notice - ✅ Suggests emoji markers - ✅ Reports non-markdown links

original.title: Step 1: Add IA-FIRST Header
original.content: Place this after your H1 title: ```markdown

original.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
original.content: - Structure: H1 → H2 (sections) → H3 (subsections) → Lists - All lists use `-` (not numbers or bullets) - All links use `[text](path.md)` format (no backticks) - All constraints marked with emoji (✅,...

original.title: Step 3: Restructure to H2/H3 Hierarchy
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
original.content: [As above]

original.title: Subtopic 2.1
original.content: - Item A - Item B - Nested detail ```

original.title: Step 4: Replace Prose with Lists
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Architecture Layers
original.content: System components: - Storage layer: handles all data persistence - Index layer: maintains vector index for similarity search - Generation layer: calls the LLM ``` ---

original.title: ✅ Validation Checklist
original.content: Before committing documentation changes: ---

original.title: Example 1: Before (Non-IA-FIRST)
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: Configuration
original.content: The system uses immutable configuration files. These files define important parameters like the maximum number of concurrent campaigns and the LLM model to use. You can't change them after startup....

original.title: Example 1: After (IA-FIRST)
original.content: ```markdown

original.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
original.content: [Standard notice...]

original.title: Immutable Configuration
original.content: Immutable config defines critical parameters: - MAX_CONCURRENT_CAMPAIGNS: limit on parallel campaigns - LLM_MODEL_VERSION: AI model selection - VECTOR_INDEX_DIMENSION: embedding dimension 1. Edit...

original.title: Mutable Configuration
original.content: Mutable config can change live without redeployment: - LLM_TEMPERATURE: within bounds [0.0, 1.0] - CACHE_TTL_SECONDS: within bounds [60, 3600] - RETRY_BACKOFF_MS: within bounds [100, 10000] 🎯...

original.title: Configuration Storage
original.content: Config locations by type: | Type | Location | Mutable? | |------|----------|----------| | Immutable | pyproject.toml | No | | Secrets | Kubernetes secrets | Admin only | | Mutable | Admin dashboard |...

original.content: - [validate-ia-first.py](../SCRIPTS/validate-ia-first.py) — Automated validation - [Quality Audit](../../../../context/runtime-state/analysis/WORLD_CLASS_REVIEW.md) — Architecture review - [Example:...

original.title: ⚠️ Common Mistakes
original.content: | Mistake | Impact | Fix | |---------|--------|-----| | Mixing prose + lists in H2 | AI confusion | Use lists only | | Backtick links `[text](path)` | Parser error | Remove backticks | | H1 → H4...

original.title: ✅ Next Steps
original.content: 1. **Audit existing docs:** `python validate-ia-first.py --audit docs/ia/` 2. **Auto-fix:** `python validate-ia-first.py --fix --audit docs/ia/` 3. **Review changes:** Check git diff 4. **Commit:**...

original.title: 📚 GUIDES ORGANIZATION
original.content: New structure for guides by purpose: ---

original.title: 🟢 ONBOARDING
original.content: When starting a new agent session: 1. **FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md** — 15-minute orientation - Understand documentation structure - Lock to ia-rules.md - Choose your PATH - Check execution state - Load...

original.title: 🔵 IMPLEMENTATION
original.content: When building features: 1. **IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md** — Step-by-step process - Full workflow from idea to merge - Checkpoints along the way - Integration points 2. **DESIGN_DECISIONS.md** — Making...

original.title: 🟣 NAVIGATION
original.content: Finding what you need: 1. **INDEX.md** — \

original.title: 🟡 CONTEXT
original.content: Background and historical information: 1. **DELIVERY_SUMMARY.md** — What was delivered - New files created - Files changed - Impact metrics 2. **FINAL_STATUS.md** — Completion checklist -...

original.title: 📋 REFERENCE
original.content: Lookup tables and definitions: 1. **FAQ.md** — Common questions and answers - \

original.title: MIGRATION NOTE
original.content: Old structure (flat): New structure (organized): Old files still exist at root level for compatibility during transition.
→ **Time:** 30 minutes → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) (15 min) 2. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (5 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 15 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) §1-3 (5 min) 2....
→ **Time:** 20 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (3 min) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` — sections 1-3 (10 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 45 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (3 min) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` — ALL sections (20 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 30 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) (15 min) 2....
→ **Read:** [CI/CD validation guide](../operational/README.md) → **Time:** 10 minutes
→ **Read:** 1. [IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md](implementation/IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md) (find similar feature) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/testing.md` (understand test types) →...
→ **Read:** 1. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/performance.md` (SLO targets) 2. [Performance SLOs](/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/performance.md) → **Time:** 20 minutes
→ **Read:** 1. Relevant ADR in `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/decisions/` 2. Project specialization in `/EXECUTION/spec/custom/rpg-narrative-server/SPECIALIZATIONS/` → **Time:** 15 minutes ---

original.title: **Backend Developer**
original.content: 1. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` (principles) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/architecture.md` (design) 3....

original.title: **DevOps / SRE**
original.content: 1. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/observability.md` (logging/metrics) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/performance.md` (SLOs) 3. [CI/CD validation...

original.title: **Product Manager**
original.content: 1. [YOUR_VISION_IMPLEMENTED.md](context/YOUR_VISION_IMPLEMENTED.md) (overview) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/backward-compatibility-policy.md` (when breaking changes allowed) 3....

original.title: **Architect**
original.content: 1. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` (ALL) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/architecture.md` (ALL) 3. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/decisions/` (ALL ADRs) -...

original.title: **5 minutes** (Quick question)
original.content: - [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) - Specific ADR title or one section

original.title: **15 minutes** (Quick decision)
original.content: - One principle from `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` - One specification from `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/` - Project specialization overview

original.title: **30 minutes** (Daily work)
original.content: - [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) - Relevant PATH from [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) - Project-specific guides

original.title: **1 hour** (In-depth understanding)
original.content: - Full `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` - Full `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/architecture.md` - Relevant ADRs (2-3)

original.title: **2+ hours** (Deep learning)
original.content: - Complete `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/` layer - Complete `/EXECUTION/spec/custom/rpg-narrative-server/` layer - Relevant guides for your role ---

original.title: **CANONICAL Layer** (Generic, all projects inherit)
original.content: Used when: Understanding principles, learning patterns, implementing across projects

original.title: **SPECIALIZATIONS Layer** (Project-specific)
original.content: Used when: Implementing rpg-narrative-server features

original.title: **Guides Layer** (Navigation & learning)
original.content: Used when: Lost, stuck, or learning ---

GUIDELINE.title: **Example 1: I want to add campaign search**

original.title: **Example 1: I want to add campaign search**
✅ Loading Optimization Checklist
original.content: When reading docs: - [ ] Know your time budget (5, 15, 30, 60+ minutes) - [ ] Know your role (backend, devops, PM, architect) - [ ] Know your task (bug fix, feature, learning) - [ ] Read ONLY what's needed
